activation_mode.mechanism: Copilot CLI rules load unconditionally at session start; no conditional or manual activation modes documented

auto_memory.mechanism: Copilot CLI does not have an AI-generated persistent memory system for rules

cross_provider_recognition.mechanism: agents_md_instructions: Copilot CLI reads AGENTS.md files following the multi-provider shared format

file_imports.mechanism: No @-import or file inclusion syntax documented for Copilot CLI rule files

hierarchical_loading.mechanism: Copilot CLI supports repo-wide instructions plus path-specific and personal instruction layers with defined precedence

providerExtensions.name: Repository-Wide Custom Instructions
providerExtensions.description: A `.github/copilot-instructions.md` file at the repository root applies to all requests made in the context of that repository. This is a distinct named file (not a generic "rules" filename) scoped to the repo root. The file is always-on and automatically included in every interaction within its scope without any explicit user invocation.

providerExtensions.name: Path-Specific Custom Instructions
providerExtensions.description: One or more `NAME.instructions.md` files placed within or below `.github/instructions/` at the repository root (or in the current working directory) apply only to requests involving files that match a specified path glob. This allows different instructions to govern different parts of a codebase — for example, separate instructions for frontend vs. backend code. When a path-specific file matches and a repository-wide file also exists, both sets of instructions are combined.

providerExtensions.name: AGENTS.md Instructions
providerExtensions.description: Copilot CLI recognizes `AGENTS.md` files following the community-owned agentsmd/agents.md standard. An `AGENTS.md` at the repository root is treated as primary instructions; `AGENTS.md` files found elsewhere (or in directories listed in the `COPILOT_CUSTOM_INSTRUCTIONS_DIRS` environment variable) are treated as additional instructions. Copilot also accepts `CLAUDE.md` and `GEMINI.md` at the repository root as equivalent instruction files. All recognized custom instruction files are combined (not priority-based fallbacks) per the source.

providerExtensions.name: Personal (Home Directory) Custom Instructions
providerExtensions.description: A file at `$HOME/.copilot/copilot-instructions.md` provides personal instructions scoped to the local user environment rather than any specific project. The `COPILOT_CUSTOM_INSTRUCTIONS_DIRS` environment variable can also point to additional directories where Copilot will look for `AGENTS.md` and path-specific `.github/instructions/**/*.instructions.md` files.
